programs.name: Medicaid Aged, Blind, and Disabled Program
programs.what_it_does: Provides medical assistance including long-term care services, nursing facility care, home-based care, and hospice for eligible seniors and disabled individuals. Covers a range of healthcare needs to support aging in place or facility care.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 65+ or disabled individuals who are low-income, with single applicants earning less than $30,276 annually and assets under $2,000.[1]
programs.benefit_value: Varies; covers premiums, long-term care, and services based on eligibility.

programs.name: Elderly and Disabled Waiver Program (EDWP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers home and community-based services (HCBS) through SOURCE and CCSP waivers to help elderly and disabled individuals avoid nursing home placement. Includes personal care, home-delivered meals, and skilled nursing to support remaining at home.[2][4]
programs.who_its_for: Nursing home-eligible elderly or functionally impaired individuals meeting Medicaid financial criteria.
programs.benefit_value: Comprehensive HCBS services tailored to prevent institutionalization.

programs.name: Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly (PACE)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers comprehensive medical and social services to frail seniors in community settings, including adult day health care and transportation. Aims to help participants live safely at home instead of in nursing facilities.
programs.who_its_for: Seniors aged 55+ who are nursing home eligible, certified by Medicaid, and reside in a PACE service area.
programs.benefit_value: All-inclusive care covering Medicare and Medicaid benefits.

programs.name: Qualified Medicare Beneficiary (QMB), Specified Low-Income Medicare Beneficiary (SLMB), Qualifying Individual (QI)
programs.what_it_does: QMB covers Medicare premiums, deductibles, and coinsurance; SLMB and QI pay Part B premiums for eligible low-income seniors. Helps reduce out-of-pocket Medicare costs through Medicaid.[1]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income Medicare beneficiaries meeting specific income and asset limits.
programs.benefit_value: Full (QMB) or partial premium/deductible coverage based on program.

programs.name: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides monthly benefits via EBT card to purchase nutritious food. Supplements food budgets for low-income households including seniors.[7]
programs.who_its_for: Low-income seniors and families meeting income guidelines.
programs.benefit_value: Varies by household size and income; average $200+ monthly per person.

programs.name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program (LIHEAP)
programs.what_it_does: Assists with heating and cooling bills, crisis assistance, and weatherization referrals. Helps low-income households manage energy costs year-round.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income households including seniors aged 60+ prioritized.
programs.benefit_value: One-time grants averaging $300-$500 for energy bills.

programs.name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
programs.what_it_does: Provides home energy efficiency upgrades like insulation and sealing to reduce utility costs. Improves health and safety through repairs.
programs.who_its_for: Low-income homeowners or renters aged 60+ prioritized.
programs.benefit_value: Free home improvements valued at thousands of dollars.

programs.name: Georgia State Health Insurance Assistance Program (Georgia SHIP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers free, objective counseling on Medicare options, appeals, and plan selection. Helps seniors navigate Medicare issues and save money.[1][3]
programs.who_its_for: All Medicare beneficiaries, especially low-income seniors.

programs.name: Home Delivered Meals (via Community Care Services Program)
programs.what_it_does: Delivers nutritious meals to homebound seniors to promote health and independence. Often includes safety wellness checks.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Homebound seniors aged 60+ unable to prepare meals.
programs.benefit_value: 5-14 meals per week based on need.

programs.name: Caregiver Respite Services (via Caregiving Programs)
programs.what_it_does: Provides temporary relief for family caregivers through respite care, adult day care, and support services. Helps prevent caregiver burnout.[3]
programs.who_its_for: Family caregivers of older adults or disabled individuals.
programs.benefit_value: Varies; hourly respite, day care sessions.

programs.name: Senior Community Service Employment Program (SCSEP)
programs.what_it_does: Offers part-time community service work and training to transition to unsubsidized employment. Provides income and skill-building for older workers.[2][3]
programs.benefit_value: Part-time wage at minimum wage, training, job placement.

programs.name: Legal Assistance for Older Georgians
programs.what_it_does: Provides free legal services, advice, and representation for civil matters affecting seniors. Covers issues like benefits, housing, and consumer problems.[3]

programs.name: Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program
programs.what_it_does: Advocates for residents in nursing homes and personal care homes to resolve complaints and improve quality of life. Provides independent investigation and mediation.[2][3]
programs.who_its_for: Residents of long-term care facilities and their families.
